title: mentha longifolia var. planitiensis
intro: Mentha longifolia var. planitiensis, also known as wild mint or horse mint, is a perennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Western Asia. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is known for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia var. planitiensis include relieving symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as easing digestive problems and menstrual cramps. This herb can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken internally for medicinal purposes.
